destinations.evidence: The pool-backed EVM builder constructs a BlockVerifier with pool, provider, EVM config, and default executor registry configuration, then passes it to RitualBlockExecutorProvider.
The non-pool build path intentionally does not install a block verifier, matching the invariant distinction.
destinations.snippet: let ritual_executor = RitualBlockExecutorProvider::new(evm_config.clone(), spc_verifier, None); // No block verifier in build_evm (no pool access)

equivalence_argument: In the nodebuilder dependency, only the transaction-pool-aware builder has enough dependencies to construct the full BlockVerifier, and it passes Some(block_verifier) into the Ritual executor provider while the no-pool path passes None.
rationale: The build_evm_with_pool path creates a BlockVerifier with async commitment validation dependencies and passes Some(block_verifier) to RitualBlockExecutorProvider::new. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: When the Ritual node builds an EVM with transaction pool access, it must construct a BlockVerifier with the pool, provider, EVM environment factory, and default executor registry contract configuration, and pass it into RitualBlockExecutorProvider.
suggested_falsification_tests: Build a node EVM with pool access, feed a block that violates a Ritual block verifier rule, and assert execution fails through the block verifier path; verify build_evm without pool does not install a block verifier.
summary: Pool-backed node EVM construction installs the Ritual block verifier into the executor provider.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Build with pool access and execute a block violating a verifier rule to assert rejection through the verifier; build without pool and assert no block verifier is installed.
title: Pool-backed executor includes block verifier
